title: Pregnancy, infant and child health supports and programs - Canada.ca
content:
## Pregnancy, infant and child health supports and programs
Supports and programs for families in Canada.
For emergency or immediate help, call 9-1-1 if your life or someone else's life is in danger.
- Provincial or territorial programs and supports
Healthy pregnancy and child health and development programs for vulnerable families, and off-reserve First Nations, Inuit and Métis families and children

### Aboriginal Head Start in Urban and Northern Communities
Early child development program for off-reserve First Nations, Inuit and Métis children and families

### Canada Prenatal Nutrition Program
Healthy pregnancy and infant health program for vulnerable pregnant people, new parents and babies

### Community Action Program for Children
Child health program for vulnerable children 0-6 years old and their families

### Healthy Early Years Program
Program to improve the healthy development of children (birth to 6 years) living in official language minority communities (Anglophones in Quebec and Francophones outside of Quebec).

### Nobody's Perfect Parenting Program
Parenting program for parents of children from birth to 5 years old, who are young, single, isolated or who have limited income or education.

## Provincial and territorial supports and programs
Child and family health programs in your province or territory.
- Family resource networks
- Breastfeeding support services
- Find a StrongStart BC program
- Healthy Start Public Health Services
- Child and youth programs
- Healthy Families, Healthy Babies
- Breastfeeding support services
Newfoundland and Labrador
- Parent and child health programs
- Breastfeeding support
Northwest Territories
- Healthy Family Program
- Breastfeeding support
- Healthy Beginnings: Enhanced home visiting
- Breastfeeding support
- Public Health pre- and post-natal clinics
- Healthy Babies, Healthy Children Program
- EarlyON Child and Family Centres
Prince Edward Island
- Public health nursing for families
- Breastfeeding drop-in clinic
- Réseau des CRP du Québec
- Breastfeeding support
- Community programs for families with young children
- Babies' and children's health programs
- Breastfeeding support
- Women's Shelters Canada
- Assaulted Women's Helpline: 1-866-863-0511 or text #SAFE (#7233)
- Canadian Mental Health Association
- First Nations and Inuit Hope for Wellness Help Line: 1- 855-242-3310 (24 hours a day, 7 days a week)
- 1- 855-242-3310 (24 hours a day, 7 days a week)
- If you or someone you know is thinking about suicide, call or text 9-8-8. Help is available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. 9-8-8: Suicide Crisis Helpline offers support that is: bilingual trauma-informed culturally appropriate available to anyone in Canada
- 9-8-8: Suicide Crisis Helpline offers support that is: bilingual trauma-informed culturally appropriate available to anyone in Canada
- culturally appropriate
- available to anyone in Canada
